page: The Amazon Web Services x8aedz.3xlarge is a memory-intensive eighth-generation server instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture server features 12 physical cores and 12 vCPUs running at a high clock speed of 5.0 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 9 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate outstanding single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression/decompression tasks placing in the top 10% tier. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks fall within the top 25% tier, supported by the fast DDR5 memory subsystem. The instance also demonstrates strong capabilities in text and image processing, as well as efficient large language model inference for text generation on smaller models. Database operations and multi-core stress tests perform at a solid average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x8aedz.3xlarge offers high resource density, combining a massive memory footprint with fast single-core execution and local NVMe storage. However, with only 12 vCPUs and a baseline network speed of 9 Gbps, it may face bottlenecks in highly parallelized compute-bound tasks or network-heavy distributed environments. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, large in-memory databases, caching layers, data processing pipelines, and localized text or image processing applications.
description: The Amazon Web Services x8aedz.3xlarge is a memory-optimized eighth-generation instance powered by a dedicated 12-core AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, providing an exceptional 32.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is supported by a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store, while network throughput is rated at a 9 Gbps baseline. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU execution, image processing, and single-threaded decompression, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth. While multi-core performance and database operations align with mid-range averages, the massive memory density and fast single-core speeds make this instance highly efficient for memory-intensive applications, in-memory databases, caching layers, and text processing workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive server featuring high-frequency AMD processors, massive DDR5 memory density, and fast local NVMe SSD storage for demanding workloads.
